summary: Applied the private Mattermost ChatOps surface live on docker-runtime-lv3, provisioned its PostgreSQL backend on postgres-lv3, published it through the Proxmox host Tailscale proxy, and verified channel bootstrap, webhook artifacts, and Grafana alert routing end to end.

verification.observed: make converge-mattermost completed successfully after rebasing implementation commit 34d0fe0f94e8b82857218bb78dadd37449e25de0 onto the current main-line release set and rerunning the full workflow without warnings or unreachable targets.

The local .local/mattermost/incoming-webhooks.json manifest reports team lv3, the alerts/approvals/handoffs/mail/workflows channels, and externally reachable managed webhook URLs through the Proxmox host Tailscale proxy.
Grafana on monitoring-lv3 exposes the lv3-mattermost-platform-alerts webhook contact point targeting http://10.10.10.20:8065/hooks/4oiuucm6x3nr3jir9m8pdgkz3r.

notes: The initial bootstrap logic had to be corrected for Mattermost's actual local mmctl behavior: JSON output can be a single object or null rather than a list, webhook list results do not expose a full URL, and local mode cannot create bot tokens.

The integrated branch also had to classify the Mattermost API and incoming webhook surfaces in config/api-publication.json so generated status documents and publication-tier validation stayed complete after rebasing onto the newer main line.
